summary: Removed the separate top-level `include` tree by moving headers into `src`, updating build/tool include paths, and resolving a few duplicate or divergent headers while doing so. The PR primarily simplified repository layout and include search configuration: `SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS` and `ASFLAGS` now point at `src`, `compile_flags.txt` dropped `-Iinclude`, formatting only scans `src`, and `gen.sh` generates/consumes context includes under `src`. Some headers were pure renames from `include` to `src`, while duplicate Dolphin headers were consolidated into the existing `src` copies, notably `src/dolphin/pad/Pad.h` and `src/dolphin/os/OSMutex.h`.
what_changed: Deleted the top-level `include` directory after moving its contents into `src`; the PR body explicitly documents the migration commands: `rsync -r --remove-source-files include/* src` and `find . -type d -empty -delete`.
Updated the Makefile include search configuration from `SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS := include include/dolphin src` to `SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS := src`.
Changed assembler include path from `ASFLAGS := -mgekko -I include` to `ASFLAGS := -mgekko -I src`.
Changed the `format` target to scan only `src` instead of both `src include`.
Removed `-Iinclude` from `compile_flags.txt`, leaving `src` as the relevant project include root for tooling.
Updated `gen.sh` so generated context include files are built from headers under `src/`, written to `src/$ctx_includes`, and passed to `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` from that location.
Deleted `include/dir.dox`, whose TODO had said to move contents to `src` and delete the directory after Makefile/tool references were fixed.
Removed duplicate include-tree headers such as `include/dolphin/base/PPCArch.h`, `include/dolphin/os/OSMutex.h`, and `include/dolphin/pad/pad.h`.
Merged declarations from `include/dolphin/pad/pad.h` into `src/dolphin/pad/Pad.h`, adding `PAD_CHANMAX`, `PAD_ERR_NONE`, `PAD_SPEC_2`, `PADStatus`, and PAD/VI function prototypes.
Changed PAD users from `<dolphin/pad/pad.h>` to `<dolphin/pad/Pad.h>` in `src/dolphin/pad/Pad.c`, `src/dolphin/pad/PadClamp.c`, and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h`.

Updated `src/dolphin/os/OSMutex.h` with declarations from the deleted include copy, including `Runtime/platform.h`, mutex function prototypes, `BOOL OSTryLockMutex`, and a field rename from `count` to `lock` at offset 0x0C.
smart_moves: The migration handled build files, editor/tool flags, formatting, assembly include paths, and m2ctx generation together, avoiding a half-migrated state where only C compilation worked.

The change preserved include spelling rooted at logical paths like `<dolphin/os/OSThread.h>` and `<Runtime/platform.h>` while changing the physical include root to `src`, minimizing churn in most source files.
Duplicate headers were not blindly kept after the move; the PR consolidated relevant declarations into existing `src` headers such as `Pad.h` and `OSMutex.h`.
The PR removed the documentation TODO file (`include/dir.dox`) only after satisfying its stated condition: move the contents and update references such as the Makefile.

The Makefile added a forward-looking TODO about separating `dolphin` and `sysdolphin` as system includes and eventually fixing quote-vs-angle include style, preserving design intent for later cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When eliminating an include root in a decomp repo, update every consumer class: compiler flags, assembler flags, IDE/clang flags, formatting scripts, context-generation scripts, and source includes.
Header moves are safest when logical include paths remain stable and only the search root changes; this limits source churn and reduces matching risk.
If both `include/foo.h` and `src/foo.h` exist, migration is an opportunity to reconcile them rather than preserve divergent duplicates.
Generated m2c/m2ctx context headers are part of the include topology; they must move with the include root or future matching work will silently use stale paths.
Build-system cleanup can unblock future header discipline, but quote includes versus system includes should be planned carefully because tools must understand the distinction.
Do not leave stale documentation or TODO directories after completing the migration; remove the marker once its task is complete.
naming_conventions: `dolphin/pad/Pad.h` became the active PAD header path, replacing the lowercase `dolphin/pad/pad.h` include path in touched users.
`PADStatus` retains official-style PAD field names such as `button`, `stickX`, `stickY`, `substickX`, `substickY`, `triggerLeft`, `triggerRight`, `analogA`, `analogB`, and `err`.
`OSMutex` field at offset 0x0C was named `lock` in the surviving header rather than `count`, matching the deleted include-tree version.

Include guards from the deleted PAD header (`_PAD_H_`) replaced the previous `DOLPHIN_PAD_PAD_H` guard in `src/dolphin/pad/Pad.h`; the evidence shows this as part of the consolidation, not as a reviewed naming rule.
Dolphin OS function declarations retained official-style names such as `OSInitMutex`, `OSLockMutex`, `OSUnlockMutex`, `__OSUnlockAllMutex`, and `OSTryLockMutex`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed assembler include root from `include` to `src` via `ASFLAGS := -mgekko -I src`, preserving access to assembly include files such as `macros.inc` after migration.
Kept `MUST_MATCH` handling unchanged under `NON_MATCHING`, so the include-root migration did not alter matching mode behavior.
Updated `gen.sh` context generation for m2c/m2ctx so all headers are discovered under `src` and the generated context include file is also stored under `src`.
Retained angle-bracket include style for moved headers while using `src` as the system include root; the Makefile TODO notes this distinction still needs future cleanup.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement the Makefile TODO for treating `dolphin` and `sysdolphin` as system includes.
Search for remaining references to `include/` or `-Iinclude` after PR #666.
Search for remaining lowercase `<dolphin/pad/pad.h>` includes after PR #666.
Investigate whether `OSMutex.lock` versus `OSMutex.count` appears in later struct-layout or matching fixes.
Find PRs that further normalize quote includes versus angle-bracket includes in `src`.
